rawCommits.subject: chore(server): log agent stream output summaries
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(specs): preserve timed-out spec drafts
rawCommits.body:
Keep streamed spec markdown recoverable when a model timeout interrupts create_spec and steer agents away from oversized spec payloads.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): fail closed on unhealthy harness sidecar
rawCommits.body:
Kill the managed local harness process when it never passes the startup health probe so the desktop does not proceed with a broken sidecar.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: fail closed on unhealthy harness sidecar

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): reduce task output persistence jank
rawCommits.body:
Reuse the IndexedDB connection across browser storage operations and debounce task-output cache writes so streaming updates do not repeatedly parse and rewrite localStorage.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): bound stale auth and harness backpressure
rawCommits.body:
Cap stale auth fallback, fail closed for sensitive routes, and make harness WebSocket commands bounded with explicit delivery errors and redacted frame logging.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: bound stale auth and harness backpressure

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): hide '/ for commands' hint in agent windows
rawCommits.body:
Floating desktop agent windows can be very narrow, so the input info
bar wraps and the orbit/environment indicators get pushed onto a
second line. Add a `compact` flag that's threaded ChatPanel ->
ChatInputBar and set on the chat surface inside AgentChatWindowPanel
to suppress the chat-mode commands hint there. The image/3d-mode
labels are kept since they convey active state, and the main chat
surface is unaffected.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hide '/ for commands' hint in agent windows

rawCommits.subject: feat(image-gen): default to gpt-image-2 and expose generate_image tool
rawCommits.body:
Promotes gpt-image-2 to the first entry in IMAGE_MODELS (so the chat
input picker and the aura3d Image Generation app both default to it),
and adds a provider-less `generate_image` workspace tool so chat agents
can fulfil natural-language requests like "create an image of a cat"
without the user having to invoke the `/image` slash command.

The new tool routes through aura-os-server, drains the upstream
aura-router SSE stream, and returns the final `completed` payload as a
single JSON value shaped for `ImageBlock` (imageUrl, originalUrl, prompt,
model, meta). Includes mock-router unit tests covering the happy path,
upstream `error` events, and missing `completed` events.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: default to gpt-image-2 and expose generate_image tool

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): keep middle panel mounted across app switches
rawCommits.body:
Lift the visible `ResponsiveMainLane` into `DesktopShell.mainPanelHost`
so the middle root panel survives app switches. Each app's `MainPanel`
now returns inner content only — the shell wraps everything in a
persistent lane that retains DOM identity across navigations, which
eliminates the size-snap flash where the wallpaper briefly showed
through before the new app's content rendered.

The new `bareMainPanel` flag on `AuraApp` lets the Desktop app keep its
transparent wallpaper-bleed surface (no lane chrome).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep middle panel mounted across app switches

rawCommits.subject: feat(server): add agent control callback endpoints
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): restore run panel task history
rawCommits.body:
Seed run panel rows from the authoritative project task list so reloads or cleared local storage do not leave completed, failed, or active runs hidden behind an empty state.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): show loop runs in Run pane regardless of viewed chat
rawCommits.body:
The Run sidekick was filtering its task list by the URL's
`agentInstanceId`, but that param is the chat agent the user is viewing
while the loop runs on a separate `Loop`-role instance. Every
`task_started` row (and every `/loop/status` rehydration) carries the
loop instance id, so the chat-scoped filter dropped them all and the
pane stayed on "No tasks" the moment Run was pressed.

The Run pane is project-scoped UI, so query the panel store by project
only and let row-level project filtering handle isolation.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: show loop runs in Run pane regardless of viewed chat

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): pass agent permissions to dev loops
rawCommits.body:
Thread normalized agent permissions into automaton start requests so dev-loop runs use the same capability bundle as chat sessions.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: pass agent permissions to dev loops

rawCommits.subject: feat(agents): add list agents capability
rawCommits.body:
Expose list_agents as a first-class agent capability so CEO and permission UI bundles can grant the harness tool explicitly.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor(server): split oversized server modules
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor(interface): clarify shell and output components
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor(interface): route server caches through queries
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): default permissions to full access
rawCommits.body:
Normalize missing or empty agent permission bundles to the canonical full-access set so harness sessions get process invocation capabilities unless permissions are explicitly narrowed.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor(interface): split stream handlers and subscriptions
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor(protocol): sync generated permission types
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor(crates): split oversized Rust modules
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): keep image models out of chat picker
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep image models out of chat picker

rawCommits.subject: refactor: route all model traffic through the Aura proxy
rawCommits.body:
Drop the `org_integration` BYOK auth path and direct provider catalog
entries (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, xAI, OpenRouter) so agents always
resolve through Aura-managed credits. Simplifies harness provider config,
billing checks, and the agent editor migration of legacy org-backed
agents, and collapses the chat model picker to a single Aura group.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: route all model traffic through the Aura proxy

rendered.title: Mobile iOS goes first-class, model traffic unifies on Aura
rendered.intro: A heavy day across the stack: a brand-new first-class iOS experience landed alongside a desktop-isolation follow-up, agents now route exclusively through the Aura proxy, and a wide reliability sweep tightened streaming, permissions, and the harness sidecar. Server and Rust internals were also reorganized to keep the platform maintainable as it grows.

entries.title: Recoverable spec drafts and richer agent stream logs
entries.summary: Streaming spec creation now survives model timeouts, and server-side agent stream output gained summary logging for easier debugging.

items.text: Streamed spec markdown is preserved when create_spec times out, so users no longer lose drafted specs to model interruptions, with guidance to keep payload sizes in check.
Agent chat streaming, persistence, and tool dispatch paths now emit output summaries to make production agent runs easier to trace.

entries.title: Desktop harness sidecar fails closed on unhealthy startup
entries.summary: The desktop app no longer continues with a broken local harness when the startup health probe never passes.

items.text: The managed local harness sidecar is now killed if it never passes its startup health check, preventing the desktop from booting into a silently broken agent runtime.

entries.title: Aura proxy becomes the only path for model traffic
entries.summary: A wide-ranging session covering a unified model-routing refactor, persistent middle panel, run-pane fixes, permission plumbing, and large internal module splits.

items.text: All chat agents now route through the Aura-managed proxy: the BYOK org_integration auth path and direct Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/xAI/OpenRouter catalog entries were removed, the chat model picker collapsed to a single Aura group, and image-only models are filtered out of the chat picker.

gpt-image-2 is now the default image model and a new provider-less generate_image tool lets chat agents fulfill natural-language image requests without needing the /image slash command, with mock-router tests covering the streaming happy path and error cases.

The middle panel is lifted into the desktop shell and stays mounted across app switches, eliminating the wallpaper-flash size snap during navigation; a new bareMainPanel flag preserves the Desktop app's transparent surface.

Run pane reliability improved: completed/failed/active runs are seeded from the authoritative project task list so reloads no longer hide history, and the pane now scopes by project so loop runs show up regardless of which chat agent is being viewed.
Task output streaming feels smoother: a shared IndexedDB connection plus debounced cache writes stop streaming updates from repeatedly parsing and rewriting localStorage.
Server hardening capped stale auth fallbacks, made sensitive routes fail closed, and bounded harness WebSocket commands with explicit delivery errors and redacted frame logging.

Agent permissions are now consistent across surfaces: dev-loop runs receive the same normalized capability bundle as chat sessions, missing permission bundles default to canonical full access, list_agents is a first-class capability, and new agent control callback endpoints expose runtime hooks.
Floating agent windows hide the '/ for commands' hint via a new compact ChatPanel/ChatInputBar flag so the input bar stops wrapping in narrow surfaces, while image/3D mode indicators remain.

Large server, crate, and interface modules were broken up — app_builder, auth_guard, error, generation, router, core entities, sessions, storage testutil, task service, and stream handlers — and billing/leaderboard caches were moved behind queries, keeping the codebase navigable as the platform grows.

media.alt: Aura proxy becomes the only path for model traffic
media.caption: The chat model picker is now a single Aura group, with image-only models filtered out of agent chats.

entries.title: CI re-trigger after harness ListAgents fix
entries.summary: A pipeline re-run picked up the new list_agents capability for the desktop sidecar build.

items.text: Desktop sidecar build was re-triggered to ensure the harness ListAgents fix is included in shipped artifacts.

entries.title: Changelog media planner gets smarter and more accurate
entries.summary: A focused pass on the changelog media tooling improved how surfaces are inferred, seeded, and validated, reducing missed or wrong screenshots in release notes.

items.text: Media planner now preserves declared targets, hardens inference seeding, broadens surface coverage, and learns from historical misses via a lessons store, while filtering out token-only candidates that produced low-signal matches.

entries.title: First-class iOS mobile views with desktop isolation
entries.summary: A dedicated mobile iOS experience shipped with its own shell, screens, and chat surfaces, followed by an isolation pass that prevents mobile changes from leaking into the desktop layout.

items.text: iOS becomes a first-class target with a Capacitor config, AppDelegate, dedicated mobile chat header and input bar, and full project screens for Agents, Files, Process, and Stats — validated against an iPhone simulator sign-off matrix covering navigation, drawers, keyboard behavior, and tab transitions.

Mobile code was relocated under interface/src/mobile (shell, navigation, screens, chat, files), with new MobileSettingsView and MobileChatPanel; the desktop SettingsView was restored after a regression where the mobile settings screen had replaced it on /projects/settings.

A mobile-boundary test and route-level isMobileLayout switch enforce that desktop routes never render mobile chrome, backed by a desktop regression sign-off matrix across agents, projects, files, tasks, run, process, stats, feed, profile, integrations, and settings.

highlights: All model traffic unified through the Aura proxy
Desktop harness sidecar fails closed on bad health
Natural-language image generation via new tool
Server and crate modules restructured for maintainability
